content.full_text:
4-77-03-006

5 April 1977

Richard Meagher
Fishkin Campaign Committee
1377 9th Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94122

Dear Mr. Meagher:

In response to your letter of March 29, 1977 concerning
how to report the receipt of old political buttons which will
in turn be sold at auctions by your committee:

Your committee must estimate the value of the buttons
contributed to the campaign and report the receipt of a non-
monetary contribution and when the buttons are sold at an
auction, the proceeds from the sale must be reported.

A notation outlining the transaction may be made on the
campaign statement to eliminate any confusion when determining
actual campaign receipts.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchina
Program Coordinator

RSK:gt

bcc: Jeanne

Fishkin Campaign Committee
1377 - 9th Avenue
San Francisco, CA. 94122
FPPC #770041

March 28, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA. 98504
916/322-5660

Dear Sirs:

Our campaign has recently received as donations a number of old
political buttons from various sources; and in turn, we sold
them at auction. Our question is which person are we required
to report as a contributor, the donor or the buyer of a button?
Reporting both would unduly inflate our real campaign receipts.

Awaiting your earliest reply.

Sincerely,

Richard M. Meagher
Campaign Manager
